assertion_results.text: Step 0 extracts the Embargo policy URL from the Security Configuration as an optional field without raising an error (§1.71 — backward compatible extraction)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md line 14: 'Embargo policy URL | https://example.com/security/embargo-policy' in the Step 0 configuration table. The configuration extraction proceeds normally without errors. embargo-check.md lines 5-6 confirm: 'The Embargo policy URL is configured in Security Configuration: Embargo policy URL: https://example.com/security/embargo-policy'. The field is treated as optional -- no error raised.

assertion_results.text: Step 1.7 evaluates the CVE severity — CVSS 7.5 (High) meets the Critical/Important threshold (CVSS >= 7.0) and triggers the embargo warning gate (§1.70)
assertion_results.evidence: embargo-check.md lines 12-19: 'CVE ID: CVE-2026-31812, CVSS Score: 7.5, Severity: High, Threshold: CVSS >= 7.0 (Critical or Important), Triggers gate?: YES -- 7.5 >= 7.0'. Lines 21-22: 'The CVSS score of 7.5 (High severity) meets the Critical/Important threshold (CVSS >= 7.0). The embargo warning gate is triggered.'

assertion_results.text: Step 1.7 presents a warning gate to the engineer including the CVE ID (CVE-2026-31812), severity level, a statement about embargo risk, and a link to the configured embargo policy URL (https://example.com/security/embargo-policy) (§1.70)
assertion_results.evidence: embargo-check.md lines 30-39: The warning gate includes: 'EMBARGO CHECK -- CVE-2026-31812 (High severity, CVSS 7.5)', 'High-severity vulnerabilities may be under embargo.', 'Before proceeding, verify with your security team that this CVE is cleared for public triage.', 'Embargo policy: https://example.com/security/embargo-policy', and 'Proceed with triage? (Yes / No)'. All four elements (CVE ID, severity level, embargo risk statement, and policy URL) are present.

assertion_results.text: Step 1.7 requires explicit engineer confirmation (Yes/No) before proceeding to Step 2 — the gate is a confirmation prompt, not an informational message (§1.70)
assertion_results.evidence: embargo-check.md line 39: 'Proceed with triage? (Yes / No)'. Lines 43-44: 'This is a confirmation prompt, not an informational message. The skill MUST wait for the engineer’s explicit response before continuing'. Lines 46-48: 'If the engineer responds "Yes": Proceed to Step 2... If the engineer responds "No": Stop execution immediately... Do not proceed to Step 2 or any subsequent step.'

assertion_results.text: The embargo warning gate does NOT trigger for Low or Moderate severity CVEs (CVSS < 7.0) — it is skipped silently when severity is below threshold (§1.70)
assertion_results.evidence: embargo-check.md lines 24-25: 'Note: For Low or Moderate severity CVEs (CVSS < 7.0), this step would be skipped silently and triage would proceed directly to Step 2. The gate only fires for Critical/Important severity (CVSS >= 7.0).'
